name: USS Cony Open Bridge
description: The exposed command position atop the Cony's superstructure — an open platform of painted steel surrounded by a waist-high windbreak, facing forward over the main gun mount. The Atlantic fog is thin tonight but persistent; the searchlight cuts a white cone into it that illuminates nothing but the fog itself. Salt spray comes over the bow in irregular pulses.
atmosphericDetails: The depth-charge rack is visible aft — a steel cradle holding the remaining Mk-84 signaling canisters, each one the size of a large thermos, painted international orange.
The sonar repeater screen is mounted inside the bridge windbreak — a green circular display showing the contact as a bright, stationary smear at bearing 210, range 800 yards.
A sound-powered telephone handset hangs on the port windbreak post, connecting the bridge to the sonar shack, the engine room, and the radio room below.
